chief_complaint: A patient presented with a giant peptic ulcer perforation caused by the physiological stress of a thyroid storm, necessitating emergent laparotomy.
history: The patient developed duodenal ulcer perforation as a result of a thyroid storm caused by Graves' thyrotoxicosis, with severely deranged thyroid hormone levels and clinical features compatible with thyroid storm based on the Burch-Wartofsky Score.
labs: Severely deranged thyroid hormone levels, high anion-gap metabolic acidosis
correct_diagnosis: Thyroid storm with giant peptic ulcer perforation
differentials: Thyroid storm with giant peptic ulcer perforation
correct_ranking: Thyroid storm with giant peptic ulcer perforation
